senses.definition: the activity or skill of making cakes or decorating cakes
examples: CakeMaking is popular in culinary schools.
She took a course in cakemaking to improve her decorating skills.

senses.definition: the process or craft of producing cakes as a business or product
examples: The café focuses on artisanal cakemaking.
Cakemaking requires precision in measurements and timing.

senses.definition: a person who bakes cakes or a person who bakes heavily on cakes in a social setting (colloquial, can be humorous or pejorative)
examples: The caker whipped up a chocolate ganache.
Don't be a caker—eat your cake in moderation.

senses.definition: multiple or plural of cake; sweet baked foods often served in slices
examples: We bought three cakes for the party.
She loves chocolate cakes.

senses.definition: a small cake or cake-like confection; a diminutive form used in some contexts
examples: She offered a cakette with tea.
The pastry shop showcased small cakettes.

senses.definition: an easy task or something very simple to achieve
examples: For him, passing the test was a cakewalk.
Compared to last year, this project is a cakewalk.

senses.definition: a procession or dance in a circle, originally a musical term from 19th century
examples: The old march included a cakewalk dance at the end.

senses.definition: past tense of cakewalk; performed an easy task or achieved something easily
examples: She cakewalked through the exam.
They cakewalked to victory in the final round.

senses.definition: a person who performs the cakewalk dance; occasionally used to refer to someone who does easy tasks
examples: The crowd cheered as the cakewalker twirled on stage.

senses.definition: plural form referring to people who perform cakewalks or to a group with easy tasks
examples: The cakewalkers took the stage one after another.

senses.definition: participating in a cakewalk; performing an easy task; moving with a light, carefree style
examples: They were cakewalking through the rehearsal.
A cakewalking performance impressed the judges.

senses.definition: plural of cakewalk; easy tasks or events; dances
examples: The tasks were mere cakewalks.
They organized several cakewalks at the fair.

senses.definition: having the taste or smell of cake; used to describe something that is cake-like in flavor
examples: The frosting had a cakey sweetness.
This sponge cake is wonderfully cakey.

senses.definition: more cake-like in taste or texture
examples: This layer is cakier than the bottom.
The batter becomes cakier when baked longer.

senses.definition: most cake-like in taste or texture
examples: This part is the cakiest crumb of the cake.

senses.definition: a word possibly sought to refer to a botanical term; in common usage, may be rare or erroneous spelling of cakey/cake-related terms
examples: The term cakile appears in some botanical lists.
Note: this item may be a misspelling or rare term.

senses.definition: the quality of being cakey; having a cake-like texture or feel
examples: The cake's cakiness makes it very soft.
Avoid overbaking to keep cakiness.

senses.definition: plural of cakiness; multiple instances of cake-like texture
examples: Different recipes show varying cakinesses.

senses.definition: the process of forming a solid layer of sugar, flour, or other material on a surface; or to accumulate cake-like substances
examples: Sugar began caking on the bowl edges.
The dough is caking around the mixer blades.

senses.definition: plural of caking; instances of forming a cake-like crust or accumulation
examples: The cakeings around the edge were uneven.

senses.definition: a term from Indian spirituality referring to energy centers in the body
examples: Meditation focuses on the crown cakra.

senses.definition: a title in Indian tradition meaning a universal ruler or great king
examples: The cakravartin is a legendary monarch in Buddhist texts.

senses.definition: rarely used variant meaning possibly cakey; not common in modern standard English
examples: This term is rarely used in everyday English.

senses.definition: short form for calorie or calendar; also a nickname or abbreviation
examples: He burned 500 calories, or cal, today.
Check the cal for the date.

senses.definition: a plant or place name; may appear in historical or regional contexts
examples: Calaba is mentioned in old travel writings.

senses.definition: a city in Nigeria or a type of red kola nut tree; can refer to the color Calabar greenish-blue or to Calabar language/classifications in historical texts
examples: Calabar is a city on the coast.
Calabar beans were used in traditional medicine.

senses.definition: historic term for people or items associated with Calabar region; may appear in historical or ethnographic text
examples: Calabari traders connected the coast with inland markets.

senses.definition: alternate spelling or historical variant of Calabari; refers to people from Calabar region
examples: Calabari traders sailed the river routes.
